methodsAttempted.query: site:univ-amu.fr Aix-Marseille Université intelligence artificielle générative ChatGPT charte guide étudiants enseignants
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced the AMU ObsiaFormation charter panorama page and AMU AI training resources.
Search found an AMU student and trainee charter PDF, AMU CIPE event pages, and faculty or event pages; only official AMU-hosted sources were retained.
Verified the ObsiaFormation subdomain as AMU-hosted and describing itself as an observatory of AI-in-training practices for Aix Marseille Université.
Site search located the AMU M3C note on AI use in evaluated student work and a guide for exploring AI in training.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found no safely fetchable central binding AI charter for Aix-Marseille University. Claims are limited to AMU-hosted ObsiaFormation guidance and a page reporting that AMU integrated an AI-use note into 2024/2025 M3C framing documents after CFVU validation. The central student charter PDF was discovered but not used as evidence because safe fetch paths returned 403 or 404.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Panorama des Chartes sur l'utilisation de l'IA - ObsiaFormation
artifacts.verificationNotes: AMU-hosted ObsiaFormation page includes a specific note that AMU integrated an AI-use mention into 2024/2025 M3C framing documents after CFVU validation.

artifacts.verificationNotes: AMU-hosted ObsiaFormation guide states its role in accompanying AI use in teaching and learning and gives non-binding good-practice guidance.

artifacts.errorReason: Official AMU PDF URL returned a 403 page to direct HTTP fetch; alternate media URL returned 404. No bypass attempted.

artifacts.claimText: Aix-Marseille University is reported by an AMU-hosted ObsiaFormation page as having integrated a 2024/2025 M3C note treating student use of AI tools such as ChatGPT in evaluated personal or group work as fraud unless expressly authorized.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI use in evaluated work is treated as fraud unless expressly authorized, per reported AMU 2024/2025 M3C note.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Panorama des Chartes sur l'utilisation de l'IA - ObsiaFormation
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Suite à une réflexion et validation en CFVU, AMU a intégré une mention dans ses documents de cadrage des Modalités de contrôle des connaissances et des compétences (M3C) pour l’année universitaire 2024/2025 : « L’utilisation par les étudiants d’outils d’intelligence artificielle (comme ChatGPT ou autre) lors de la production de travaux personnels ou de groupe de toute nature, susceptible de faire l’objet d’une évaluation, est considérée comme une fraude passible de poursuites disciplinaires, à moins qu’elle ne soit expressément autorisée.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: After CFVU validation, AMU integrated a 2024/2025 M3C note treating student AI use in evaluated work as fraud unless expressly authorized.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section titled "Note sur les M3C et AMU"
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guide d'accompagnement : explorer l'IA en formation - ObsiaFormation
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Parmi les bonnes pratiques : Ne pas surcharger les étudiants avec trop d’automatisation, privilégier une approche équilibrée. Vérifier la pertinence et la fiabilité des outils et de leurs contributions avant de les intégrer. Respecter les principes d’éthique, notamment en matière de protection des données.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guide lists good practices including balanced use, checking tool reliability and outputs, and respecting ethics including data protection.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section titled "Quelles sont les bonnes pratiques et erreurs à éviter ?"

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow claim based on the AMU-hosted ObsiaFormation statement about M3C framing documents; not elevated to a full central AI charter claim.
Approved because the cited M3C note directly states the disclosure expectation for expressly authorized AI use.
Approved as guidance-scoped source-status/teaching support, not binding policy.
Approved as non-binding guide content; claim wording uses 'frames' and does not impose mandatory requirements.

artifacts.title: Aix-Marseille University AI Policy Staging Notes
artifacts.summary: No safely fetchable central binding AI policy page or AI charter was found for Aix-Marseille University in this pass. The staged claims rely on AMU-hosted ObsiaFormation sources: one reports a CFVU-validated M3C note for 2024/2025 treating unauthorized student AI use in evaluated work as fraud and requiring explicit mention when authorized; another provides non-binding teaching guidance about exploring AI in formation, checking reliability, balanced use, ethics, and data protection.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The central AMU student and trainee charter PDF was discovered but could not be safely fetched; it is recorded as inaccessible and not used for evidence.
ObsiaFormation guide claims are guidance-scoped and should not be read as binding university-wide policy.
No claim in this staged bundle is marked canonical or human-reviewed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
